content.text:
<user_query>
For test builds in layered products job, add a banner to the job run that says test. Look at what we are doing in OCP for Konflux for reference.
</user_query>
Let me research the relevant code to understand the layered products job and how OCP Konflux builds handle test banners.

input.prompt: Search the art-tools repository for the layered products build pipeline/job. Look in pyartcd/pipelines/ for anything related to "layered" products builds. Return the file paths found and a summary of their contents, particularly any test-related logic or banner/slack messaging.

input.description: Find OCP Konflux test banner logic
input.prompt: Search the art-tools repository (specifically pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/) for how OCP Konflux builds handle "test" builds - look for test banners, jenkins.update_title with "TEST", or any logic that marks a build as a test build. Check ocp4.py, ocp4_scan.py, ocp4_scan_konflux.py, and any other relevant OCP build pipelines. Also check the jenkins.py file for update_title and update_description patterns. Return the relevant code snippets showing how test banners are implemented.

content.text: Now let me look at the specific code in the layered products build pipeline and the OCP reference for test banners.
Now let me look at the OCP Brew pipeline (`ocp.py`) to see the exact test banner pattern:
Now let me also check what the OCP4 Konflux pipeline does at initialization to understand the full pattern:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me also check if the OCP Konflux pipeline has a similar `[TEST]` check, since the user mentioned "look at what we are doing in OCP for Konflux":

Now I have all the context needed. The OCP Brew pipeline (`ocp.py`) has an explicit `[TEST]` banner at line 109-110, but neither the OCP Konflux pipeline (`ocp4_konflux.py`) nor the layered products build pipeline (`build_layered_products.py`) have this banner. The user wants to add the same `[TEST]` banner to the layered products build job.

input.name: Test banner layered products
input.overview: Add a `[TEST]` Jenkins banner to the layered products build job when the assembly is "test", following the same pattern used in the OCP Brew pipeline (`ocp.py`).
input.plan:
# Add [TEST] Banner to Layered Products Build Job

## Reference Pattern

The OCP Brew pipeline already does this in [`pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/ocp.py`](pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/ocp.py) (lines 109-110):

```109:110:pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/ocp.py
        if self.assembly.lower() == "test":
            jenkins.update_title(" [TEST]")
```

## Change

Add the same check in [`pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/build_layered_products.py`](pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/build_layered_products.py), inside the `__init__` method right after `jenkins.init_jenkins()` (line 67). This is where Jenkins is initialized, making it the natural place to set the title banner -- consistent with how `ocp.py` sets it during its initialization phase (`_check_assembly`).

The addition at line 67-68:

```python
jenkins.init_jenkins()
if self.assembly.lower() == "test":
    jenkins.update_title(" [TEST]")
```

## Test Update

Add a unit test in [`pyartcd/tests/pipelines/test_build_layered_products.py`](pyartcd/tests/pipelines/test_build_layered_products.py) to verify:
- When `assembly='test'`, `jenkins.update_title(" [TEST]")` is called during initialization
- When `assembly='stream'` (or any non-test value), `jenkins.update_title` is not called

The test will mock `jenkins.init_jenkins` and `jenkins.update_title` and instantiate the pipeline with `assembly='test'` vs `assembly='stream'`.

todos.content: Add unit test verifying the [TEST] banner is set when assembly is 'test' and not set otherwise
